recipes.title: Empanadas de Pino
recipes.description: Baked Chilean empanadas filled with seasoned ground beef, onions, hard-boiled egg, olives and raisins.
steps: Mix flour and salt, rub in melted lard, then add warm water to form a smooth dough. Rest 30 minutes.
Sauté onions until translucent, add ground beef, cumin, paprika, salt and pepper, cook until browned.
Cool the pino filling completely and refrigerate so the fat sets and it's easy to handle.
Roll dough thin and cut 15 cm circles.
Place a spoonful of pino, an olive, a few raisins, and a quarter of egg on each circle.
Fold into a half-moon, seal edges with water, and tuck the corners to form the classic empanada shape.
Brush with egg yolk and bake at 200°C for 20-25 minutes until deep golden.

recipes.title: Pastel de Choclo
recipes.description: Chilean corn pie with a spiced beef pino base, chicken, olives and raisins, topped with a sweet ground corn crust.
steps: Blend corn kernels with milk and basil until smooth, then cook with butter until thickened. Season with salt.
Sauté onions, add ground beef, cumin and paprika, cook until browned.
Layer the pino in individual clay dishes, then add shredded chicken, olives, raisins and egg slices.
Cover with the corn purée and smooth the top.
Sprinkle with sugar and bake at 200°C for 25-30 minutes until the top is golden and caramelised.

recipes.title: Cazuela
recipes.description: Hearty Chilean broth-based stew with beef or chicken, pumpkin, corn, potato and rice.

ingredients.name: fresh corn on the cob, in rounds

steps: Place meat, onion and water in a large pot, season with salt and simmer 45 minutes, skimming foam.
Add potatoes, pumpkin and carrot and cook 15 minutes.
Add corn rounds, green beans and rice and cook another 15 minutes until rice is tender.
Adjust seasoning, finish with chopped cilantro and serve a piece of meat with broth and vegetables in each bowl.

recipes.title: Curanto
recipes.description: Chiloé island feast of shellfish, meats, sausages, potatoes and milcao traditionally cooked over hot stones in an earth pit.
steps: Mix grated and mashed potato with flour and salt to form milcao and chapalele dough patties.
In a large pot, layer chicken, ribs and sausages on the bottom, pour in wine and a little water.
Add potatoes and the milcao and chapalele patties on top of the meats.
Top with mussels and clams and cover everything with cabbage leaves to trap steam.
Cover tightly and steam for 45-60 minutes until shellfish open and meats are cooked through.
Serve in deep bowls with the resulting broth ladled over.

recipes.title: Completo
recipes.description: Chilean-style hot dog loaded with diced tomato, mashed avocado, sauerkraut and mayonnaise.
steps: Boil the frankfurters in water for 5 minutes until heated through.
Warm the buns briefly in the oven or steamer.
Place a sausage in each bun and top with diced tomato, then sauerkraut.
Spread mashed avocado on top, season with salt, and finish with a generous swirl of mayonnaise.

recipes.title: Humitas
recipes.description: Chilean fresh corn parcels seasoned with basil and onion, wrapped in corn husks and steamed.
steps: Sauté onion in butter with paprika until soft, then cool.
Blend corn kernels and basil into a thick paste; mix with the cooked onion and salt.
Place two overlapping husks, spoon corn mixture into the centre, fold sides over and tie with husk strips.
Steam the parcels upright for 45 minutes until firm.
Serve hot, traditionally with a sprinkle of sugar or fresh tomato salad.

recipes.title: Asado Chileno
recipes.description: Chilean-style grilled meats including beef, pork ribs, longaniza and chicken cooked over wood embers.
steps: Light a wood or charcoal fire and let it burn down to glowing embers.
Generously salt all the meats just before grilling.
Grill the slow pieces (ribs) first over medium heat, turning occasionally, for 60-90 minutes.
Add the chicken and sausages to the grill and cook for 25-35 minutes, turning until the skin is crisp and juices run clear.
Rest the meats briefly, slice and serve with lemon wedges, pebre and crusty bread.

recipes.title: Sopaipillas
recipes.description: Chilean fried pumpkin dough discs, often served with pebre or drenched in chancaca syrup in winter.
steps: Mix warm mashed pumpkin with melted lard, then add flour, baking powder and salt to form a soft dough.
Rest the dough 20 minutes, then roll out 5 mm thick.
Cut 8 cm circles and prick each with a fork.
Heat oil to 180°C and fry sopaipillas in batches until puffed and golden, about 2 minutes per side.
Drain on paper and serve warm with pebre or with chancaca syrup for sopaipillas pasadas.

recipes.title: Pebre
recipes.description: Chilean fresh herb and chilli salsa with onion, tomato, cilantro and ají, served with bread or grilled meats.

ingredients.name: ají verde or green chilli, minced

steps: Soak the diced onion in cold water for 5 minutes, then drain to mellow its bite.
Combine onion, tomato, cilantro, chilli and garlic in a bowl.
Stir in olive oil, vinegar and salt and let rest 15 minutes for flavours to meld.
Serve with crusty bread, sopaipillas or grilled meats.

recipes.title: Pollo a la Plancha
recipes.description: Chilean-style griddled chicken breast marinated in garlic, lemon and oregano, served with salad or rice.
steps: Pound the chicken breasts to even thickness.
Mix garlic, lemon juice, oregano, oil, salt and pepper, and marinate the chicken for at least 30 minutes.
Heat a flat griddle or heavy pan over medium-high heat with a thin film of oil.
Sear the chicken 4-5 minutes per side until golden and cooked through.
Rest 3 minutes, then serve with rice, salad or pebre.

recipes.title: Charquicán
recipes.description: Chilean mashed stew of beef, pumpkin, potato, corn and green beans, often topped with a fried egg.
steps: Brown the beef in a heavy pot, then remove.
Sauté onion and garlic with cumin and paprika until soft.
Return the beef and add pumpkin, potatoes and stock; simmer 25 minutes.
Add corn and green beans and cook 10 more minutes until everything is tender.
Mash roughly with a wooden spoon to a chunky purée and adjust seasoning.
Top each serving with a fried egg, if desired.

recipes.title: Porotos Granados
recipes.description: Chilean summer stew of fresh cranberry beans, pumpkin, corn and basil.
steps: Sauté onion and garlic in olive oil with paprika until soft.
Add tomatoes and cook 5 minutes until they break down.
Add beans, pumpkin and water and simmer 30 minutes until beans are tender.
Add corn and a handful of chopped basil and cook 10 more minutes; the pumpkin should partly dissolve to thicken the stew.
Adjust seasoning and serve with extra basil on top.
